description: Simplification rules applied to both C and Rust common ASTs. These normalize syntax differences without losing semantic information. Applied bottom-up, leaves first.

simplification_rules.name: c_camel_to_snake
simplification_rules.justification: C uses camelCase, Rust uses snake_case. Same identifiers.

simplification_rules.name: c_field_camel_to_snake
simplification_rules.justification: Field names: C camelCase → Rust snake_case.

simplification_rules.name: c_break_in_arm
simplification_rules.justification: C switch cases need explicit break. Rust match arms end implicitly.

simplification_rules.name: c_strip_parser_prefix
simplification_rules.justification: C accesses fields via parser->m_field. Rust uses self.field.

simplification_rules.name: c_strip_m_prefix
simplification_rules.justification: C uses m_ prefix for struct members. Rust doesn't.

simplification_rules.name: c_normalize_arm_labels_tok
simplification_rules.justification: C case XML_TOK_X → Rust arm XmlTok::X

simplification_rules.name: c_normalize_arm_labels_role
simplification_rules.justification: C case XML_ROLE_X → Rust arm Role::X

simplification_rules.name: rust_strip_self_prefix
simplification_rules.justification: Rust self.field — keep self as child but already normalized.
